items.title: McDonald’s is struggling to hold on to its low-income customers
items.description: Lower-income customers aren’t finding much value in the menu at McDonald’s these days. Traffic dropped “nearly double digits” industry-wide among this demographic, chief executive Christopher Kempczinski told investors Wednesday during the fast-food giant’s third-quarter earnings call. Such customers are disproportionately feeling the strain of mounting economic pressures, including higher prices on groceries and apparel as well as the growing cost of rent and child care, he said.Subscribe to Th

items.title: How major US stock indexes fared Wednesday, 11/5/2025
items.description: Stocks gained ground on Wall Street following several upbeat economic updates and a steady flow of quarterly reports from U.S. companies. The S&P 500 rose 0.4% Wednesday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.5%, and the Nasdaq composite added 0.6%.
